title: Layered Undo with Restore, Reset, Revert, and References · Recovery Case: Gita Undo Layer Classification
summary: An incident spans unstaged edits, staged policy, three local commits, one published merge, and a release tag; each layer requires a different reversible repair. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita undo layer classification increment in layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Undo Layer Classification
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G15 is a theory in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Undo Layer Classification contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Undo Layer Classification while producing layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G15 is a worked example in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity. Competency probe: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation. A defensible solution applies Gita Undo Layer Classification, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-undo-layer-classification — Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available
git reset --mixed HEAD^
# expected: HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction
# observation: the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable
# changed-case: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity
# transfer-limit: preview untracked and ignored data separately before any real hard reset or clean operation
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Undo Layer Classification evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-undo-layer-classification-1tadwt" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Undo Layer Classification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-undo-layer-classification-1tadwt
# scenario-key: trace-1tadwt probe-twdat1 boundary-adwt1t owner-1tadwtx fault-1tadwtz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1t candidate=b00b1wt
# hypothesis: only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available
git reset --mixed HEAD^
# expected: HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction
# observation: the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable
# changed-case: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history
# after-ref: main=a11ce1t candidate=c0ffeet
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity
# transfer-limit: preview untracked and ignored data separately before any real hard reset or clean operation
